Title: A computational workflow for the expansion of heterologous biosynthetic pathways to natural product derivatives

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosylmethionine to hydroxyl groups on benzylisoquinoline alkaloid substrates; directly methylates (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine to (S)-tetrahydropalmatine in the engineered system.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Derivative branch from noscapine intermediate (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine; also armepavine/laudanine side branches
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Analgesic, anxiolytic, sedative; potential treatment for opiate addiction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes de novo (S)-tetrahydropalmatine production in engineered yeast; also enables production of (S)-armepavine and (S)-laudanine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BridgIT-ranked CjColOMT was experimentally validated as an active enzyme for methylating (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine to (S)-tetrahydropalmatine. 2. In vivo expression in yeast increased (S)-tetrahydropalmatine about eight-fold versus empty plasmid control. 3. In vitro CjColOMT showed over 11-fold higher conversion of (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine than yPsS9OMT. 4. CjColOMT also accepted multiple related BIA substrates, producing (S)-armepavine and (S)-laudanine and multiple products from (S)-scoulerine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for engineered derivative branch; not established as native rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed from a high-copy plasmid in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-Ura selective medium; 2% dextrose; 2 mM L-DOPA; 10 mM ascorbic acid; 25–30 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported in vitro promiscuity toward (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine existed in literature, but KEGG lacked sequence annotation for this orphan reaction. CjColOMT showed broad BIA substrate promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BridgIT prediction; codon-optimized heterologous expression in S. cerevisiae; de novo metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS; purified enzyme in vitro assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous module for microbial production of tetrahydropalmatine and other BIA derivatives; promising target for stacking with upstream noscapine/BIA pathways and further protein engineering to boost titer
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad promiscuity may generate by-products from related BIA intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosylmethionine to BIA hydroxyl groups; promiscuously methylates (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine to (S)-tetrahydropalmatine.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid/noscapine pathway and derivative branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Native scoulerine methylation step and off-target tetrahydropalmatine branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream in native pathway; side-route for derivative production
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product via promiscuous side activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Analgesic, anxiolytic, sedative; potential anti-addiction therapeutic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers background de novo (S)-tetrahydropalmatine production in engineered yeast and is active in vitro on (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. yPsS9OMT was predicted by BridgIT as a candidate methyltransferase for tetrahydropalmatine formation. 2. Among pathway methyltransferases tested, only yPsS9OMT produced (S)-tetrahydropalmatine in vitro from (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine. 3. Deleting yPsS9OMT together with absence of CjColOMT eliminated tetrahydropalmatine production in vivo, indicating it is the source of background production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Integrated into yeast genome as codon-optimized gene
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-Ura; 2% dextrose; 2 mM L-DOPA; 10 mM ascorbic acid; 25–30 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a promiscuous catalyst on tetrahydrocolumbamine; lower activity than CjColOMT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vivo pathway deletion/addition tests in yeast; purified enzyme in vitro assay; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be leveraged as a native-pathway enzyme with useful side activity for tetrahydropalmatine biosynthesis; candidate for further engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower catalytic efficiency than CjColOMT; promiscuity may alter flux among BIA intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promiscuously methylates the 7-hydroxyl group of (S)-N-methylcoclaurine to form (S)-armepavine in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled de novo production of (S)-armepavine in engineered yeast; no detectable activity on (S)-reticuline to form (S)-laudanine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BridgIT identified CjN6OMT among top candidate enzymes for armepavine biosynthesis. 2. Heterologous expression in a de novo (S)-N-methylcoclaurine-producing yeast strain yielded the desired derivative. 3. The enzyme showed substrate selectivity, producing armepavine but not laudanine from reticuline.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed from a high-copy plasmid in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-Ura; 2% dextrose; 2 mM L-DOPA; 10 mM ascorbic acid; 25 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Substrate discrimination contrasted with PsHNC4'OMT, which showed the opposite activity pattern on related substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BridgIT prediction; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS/MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme module for armepavine branch construction and selective BIA diversification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate specificity may limit versatility across related BIA intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promiscuously methylates the 7-hydroxyl group of (S)-reticuline to form (S)-laudanine in the engineered yeast pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled de novo production of (S)-laudanine in engineered yeast; did not show corresponding armepavine-forming activity on (S)-N-methylcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsHNC4'OMT was among top BridgIT-predicted enzymes for laudanine formation. 2. Expression in a reticuline-producing yeast strain yielded the desired product. 3. The enzyme displayed selective activity opposite to CjN6OMT on two closely related substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed from a high-copy plasmid in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-Ura; 2% dextrose; 2 mM L-DOPA; 10 mM ascorbic acid; 25 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights opposite substrate preference relative to CjN6OMT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BridgIT prediction; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS/MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful selective methyltransferase for laudanine branch engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Selective activity may require tailored pathway context

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylenedioxy ring formation from vicinal hydroxyl/methoxyl groups on (S)-scoulerine to form (S)-nandinine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly enabled de novo (S)-nandinine production in engineered yeast; activity was over 100-fold higher than PsCYP719A21
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcCYP719A3 was one of the top BridgIT-predicted enzymes for nandinine formation. 2. Heterologous expression in a scoulerine-producing yeast strain produced (S)-nandinine. 3. Its activity in vivo was more than 100-fold higher than PsCYP719A21.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed from a high-copy plasmid in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-Ura; 2% dextrose; 2 mM L-DOPA; 10 mM ascorbic acid; 25 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported in vitro activity on scoulerine was consistent with in vivo activity observed here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BridgIT prediction; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS/MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for microbial production of methylenedioxy BIA derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: P450 expression can be host-context dependent; exact redox partner requirements not detailed here

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylenedioxy bridge formation on (S)-scoulerine to form (S)-nandinine in vivo in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled detectable de novo (S)-nandinine production in engineered yeast, though much weaker than EcCYP719A3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCYP719A21 was predicted by BridgIT as a candidate for nandinine biosynthesis. 2. In vivo expression in a scoulerine-producing yeast strain gave detectable nandinine. 3. Its activity was far lower than EcCYP719A3 and differed from prior in vitro reports.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed from a high-copy plasmid in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-Ura; 2% dextrose; 2 mM L-DOPA; 10 mM ascorbic acid; 25 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported to lack scoulerine activity in vitro previously, but showed in vivo activity here, emphasizing context dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BridgIT prediction; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS/MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for nandinine branch engineering; may benefit from host/context optimization
